query: France Ebola case 2026: current status of the doctor who tested positive after aid mission, any secondary cases, ECDC reporting. How many confirmed Ebola cases in France as of July 2026?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's the key takeaway:

**As of July 2026, France has exactly 1 (one) confirmed Ebola case** — the imported case in an ALIMA humanitarian doctor who returned from the DRC and tested positive on 24 June 2026.

**Current status of the doctor:** RECOVERED and DISCHARGED. France's Health Minister Stéphanie Rist announced on 4 July 2026 that the patient had recovered and left the hospital, confirmed by two negative PCR tests. The patient was nearly asymptomatic (only headaches).

**Secondary cases:** NONE. No secondary cases occurred in France or anywhere in the EU/EEA. Five flight passengers were placed in precautionary isolation, but no onward transmission resulted.

**ECDC reporting:** ECDC's regularly updated outbreak page (last updated 14 July 2026) explicitly lists the single France imported case, confirms zero secondary EU/EEA cases, and assesses the infection risk for people in the EU/EEA as "very low." WHO's DON also lists "one from France (linked to DRC)." The case is part of the broader 2026 Bundibugyo ebolavirus epidemic in DRC/Uganda (1,963 DRC cases and 719 deaths as of mid-July).
